message:
fix(string): populate deprecated _sys_siglist with the same descriptions as sys_siglist

`_sys_siglist[NSIG]` is the leading-underscore historical alias of
glibc's `sys_siglist[NSIG]` array. Old C programs index it directly
when they want human-readable signal descriptions, so it MUST contain
the same populated table as `sys_siglist` — not the null placeholder
that previously lived in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs`.

Three coordinated changes:

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs`:

- Refactor the existing `sys_siglist` body so the 65-entry signal
  description table is named once as a private `const`:

      const SYS_SIGLIST_ENTRIES: [*const c_char; SYS_SIGLIST_LEN] = [
          SIG_DESC_EMPTY.as_ptr() as *const c_char,  // 0
          SIG_DESC_HUP.as_ptr()   as *const c_char,  // 1 SIGHUP
          ...
          SIG_DESC_RT.as_ptr()    as *const c_char,  // 62..=64
      ];

  Both `sys_siglist` and the new `_sys_siglist` then wrap that const
  via `SysSigList(SYS_SIGLIST_ENTRIES)`. No runtime indirection — the
  linker still sees two flat exported arrays of `*const c_char`.

- Define the previously-missing `_sys_siglist` here, with
  `#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]` so it
  exports with the historical glibc symbol name. Doc comment notes
  that old C programs index it directly and a null placeholder
  silently breaks them.

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs`:

- Remove the stale tentative-null definition

      pub static mut _sys_siglist: *const *const c_char = std::ptr::null();

  which was the placeholder that caused the regression. The
  canonical populated symbol now lives in `string_abi.rs`.

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/string_abi_test.rs`:

- Import `_sys_siglist` alongside `sys_siglist`.
- Add `read_under_sys_siglist_str(idx)` mirror of the existing
  `read_sys_siglist_str(idx)` reader.
- New test `under_sys_siglist_has_65_populated_entries` asserts
  `_sys_siglist.0.len() == sys_siglist.0.len()` and that every
  index produces the same C-string bytes through both symbols, so
  any future drift between the two arrays is caught by the test
  matrix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5498,22 +5498,7 @@ pub struct SysSigList(pub [*const c_char; SYS_SIGLIST_LEN]);
 // SAFETY: see SysSigList docs above.
 unsafe impl Sync for SysSigList {}
 
-/// glibc `sys_siglist[NSIG]` — array of human-readable signal
-/// descriptions indexed by signal number. Deprecated in favor of
-/// [`strsignal`] / `sigdescr_np`, but many older programs still
-/// reference this symbol directly. Each entry is a NUL-terminated
-/// C string with the same wording as [`strsignal(n)`].
-///
-/// `sys_siglist[0]` is empty (no signal 0 description). Indices
-/// 32..=64 cover the realtime-signal range and share a generic
-/// placeholder description.
-///
-/// The wrapper around the inner `[*const c_char; 65]` is
-/// `repr(transparent)`, so the symbol's ABI is identical to a
-/// bare C `const char *sys_siglist[NSIG]`.
-#[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
-#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
-pub static sys_siglist: SysSigList = SysSigList([
+const SYS_SIGLIST_ENTRIES: [*const c_char; SYS_SIGLIST_LEN] = [
     SIG_DESC_EMPTY.as_ptr() as *const c_char,  // 0
     SIG_DESC_HUP.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 1 SIGHUP
     SIG_DESC_INT.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 2 SIGINT
@@ -5580,7 +5565,31 @@ pub static sys_siglist: SysSigList = SysSigList([
     SIG_DESC_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 62
     SIG_DESC_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 63
     SIG_DESC_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 64
-]);
+];
+
+/// glibc `sys_siglist[NSIG]` — array of human-readable signal
+/// descriptions indexed by signal number. Deprecated in favor of
+/// [`strsignal`] / `sigdescr_np`, but many older programs still
+/// reference this symbol directly. Each entry is a NUL-terminated
+/// C string with the same wording as [`strsignal(n)`].
+///
+/// `sys_siglist[0]` is empty (no signal 0 description). Indices
+/// 32..=64 cover the realtime-signal range and share a generic
+/// placeholder description.
+///
+/// The wrapper around the inner `[*const c_char; 65]` is
+/// `repr(transparent)`, so the symbol's ABI is identical to a
+/// bare C `const char *sys_siglist[NSIG]`.
+#[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub static sys_siglist: SysSigList = SysSigList(SYS_SIGLIST_ENTRIES);
+
+/// glibc deprecated `_sys_siglist[NSIG]` alias. It must contain the
+/// same populated signal-description table as `sys_siglist`, not a
+/// null placeholder, because old C programs index this symbol directly.
+#[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub static _sys_siglist: SysSigList = SysSigList(SYS_SIGLIST_ENTRIES);
 
 // Per-signal short name bytes used by `sys_signame` — uppercase
 // without the "SIG" prefix, matching the BSD convention used by
